Document:
# One-Handed Melee
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Melee_Weapons"]
One-handed melee weapons are fast, easy to wield, and leave your other hand free for a shield, tool, or secondary weapon. They are ideal for close-quarters combat and self-defense.
## Knives
The most basic of survival tools — useful for crafting, hunting, and fighting.
[bold]Combat Knife[/bold] — Military-grade fighting knife. Fast, light, and deadly in trained hands.
[bold]Kitchen Knife[/bold] — Common blade found in any pre-war ruin. Better than bare fists.
[bold]Tribal Knife[/bold] — Handmade tribal blade. Rough but effective.
[bold]Butcher's Cleaver[/bold] — Heavy chopping blade. Slow but deals serious wound damage.
## Hatchets and Axes
Fast and reliable. A staple tool and weapon throughout the Wasteland.
[bold]Hatchet[/bold] — Compact one-handed axe. Doubles as a tool and a weapon.
[bold]Tribal Hatchet[/bold] — Hand-forged hatchet with tribal markings. Light and quick.
[bold]Fire Axe[/bold] — Heavy emergency axe. Slower swing but excellent damage.
## Batons and Clubs
Non-lethal options for subduing enemies, or improvised weapons when nothing else is available.
[bold]Police Baton[/bold] — Standard-issue law enforcement baton. Good for subduing without killing.
[bold]Tribal Club[/bold] — Crude but effective blunt weapon. Common among tribal warriors.
[bold]Broken Pipe[/bold] — Scavenged plumbing turned weapon. Desperate but functional.
## Short Blades and Swords
One-handed blades that deal more damage than knives while remaining swift.
[bold]Machete[/bold] — The Wasteland's favourite all-purpose blade. Fast, reliable, and easy to maintain.
[bold]Scrap Machete[/bold] — Improvised machete made from junk metal. Rougher edge but still cuts.
[bold]Chinese Sword[/bold] — Pre-war military officer's blade. Well-balanced and sharp.
[bold]Ceremonial Sword[/bold] — Decorative but functional blade. Often carried by faction officers.
